rationale: The two papers share a meaningful domain overlap (digital ad context effects) and both involve trust-related outcomes: the citing paper centers on brand trust erosion from unsafe adjacency, while the cited paper reports native-ad trade-offs including website trustworthiness. That makes the citation plausibly more than a generic mention (e.g., prior mechanism/context evidence), but the provided evidence includes no in-text citation context beyond a DOI match. Given this sparse linkage, it is not safe to drop, but not strong enough to clearly keep.
